acceptance_criteria.text: The repository shall not contain the onlook/ or penpot/ directories after this task.
When `git status` is run, the system shall report no remaining onlook references.
While the build process runs, the system shall not import any onlook modules.
If any reference to onlook remains in package.json or imports, the lint script shall fail.

tickets.title: 既存実装インベントリ監査 (39 routers + 54 services + 7 migrations) → existing-inventory.json
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The audit shall produce existing-inventory.json listing every backend/routers/*.py, backend/services/*.py, and data/db migration with classification (REUSE / REFACTOR / ARCHIVE / NEW / UNDETERMINED) and a 1-line rationale.
When the audit runs, the system shall cross-reference each file with tickets.json existing_files and report tickets whose listed files do not exist in the repo.
While the audit is running, the system shall not modify any source file (read-only) and shall not require external network.
Where penpot_client.py / browser_use_service.py are present, the audit shall annotate them with the Phase boundary (Phase 1.5 / Phase 2) under which they will be REFACTORed or ARCHIVEd.
If a router/service cannot be confidently classified, the audit shall record it as UNDETERMINED with the specific reason, instead of silently defaulting to REUSE.

tickets.title: UNDETERMINED 64 件 + Orphan 6 件のチケット紐付けクリーンアップ
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The cleanup shall reduce existing-inventory.json UNDETERMINED count to zero or annotate each remaining file with a phase_boundary (Phase 1.5 / Phase 2) and explicit reason.
When a file matches an existing REFACTOR ticket by basename or feature, the system shall add the file's exact path to that ticket's existing_files.
While the cleanup is running, the system shall not introduce duplicate entries to any ticket's existing_files.
Where multiple tickets could plausibly own a file, the cleanup shall pick the highest-specificity ticket (REFACTOR > REUSE > NEW) and document the choice.
If a file cannot be mapped with confidence, the cleanup shall mark it as triage_needed with a 1-line reason instead of silently assigning to the closest ticket.

acceptance_criteria.text: The claude-runner shall spawn Claude Code as a subprocess via claude-agent-sdk and capture all stdout/stderr.
When a task starts, the system shall create a session row with status=running and stream logs to session_logs.
While the runner is active, the system shall send heartbeat every 30s; absent heartbeat marks the session crashed.
If the subprocess crashes, the system shall mark status=crashed and offer 4-choice resume (checkpoint/rerun/manual/cancel).

tickets.title: RLS helper (既存 auth_middleware.py 拡張)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall execute all bash commands inside an OS-level sandbox (Seatbelt on macOS, Landlock+seccomp on Linux).
When a sandboxed process attempts to write outside the workspace path, the system shall block the syscall.
While running in sandbox, the system shall deny network access except to listed allowlist hosts.
If a process attempts to escape the sandbox, the system shall kill the session and log a redline event.

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall organize backend code into 13 bounded-context domain modules.
Each domain module shall expose only its public interface to other modules.
When a domain imports another domain's internal model, the linter shall report a violation.
If circular dependencies are detected between domains, the build shall fail.

tickets.title: 認証 6 テーブル DDL (existing initial_schema.sql 拡張)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall integrate Supabase Auth (GoTrue) for all user authentication flows.
When a user submits valid credentials, the system shall return a JWT and refresh token within 500ms.
While 2FA is enabled, the system shall require a TOTP code on every new device login.
If the JWT is expired or tampered, the system shall return 401 and shall not grant access.

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall apply request-id, CORS, rate-limit, and auth middleware to every request.
When a request enters the API, the system shall assign a unique request-id and propagate it through all logs.
While a request is rate-limited, the system shall return 429 with retry-after header.
If a request fails authentication middleware, the system shall not reach domain handlers.

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall enforce Row Level Security (RLS) on all 43 user-data tables.
When a user queries a row outside their workspace, the system shall return zero rows.
While operating as service_role, the system shall bypass RLS only in trusted backend code.
Where the user is account_owner, the system shall grant cross-workspace read access within their account.
If RLS is disabled on any user-data table, the migration test suite shall fail.

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall support spawning 4, 9, 16, or 64 parallel sessions in a single swarm pool.
When a swarm is started, the system shall allocate one git worktree + one branch per session to prevent file conflicts.
While a swarm is running, the system shall display per-cell live log previews and aggregate stats (queued/running/done/failed).
If two sessions in the same swarm modify the same file, the system shall not allow both to commit; the second shall be marked conflicted.

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall maintain a 3-tier memory: short (raw), mid (compressed + 9-section summary), long (Mem0 + Obsidian + Constitution).
When the context window reaches 95%, the system shall auto-generate the 9-section structured summary and replace older raw messages.
While prompt caching is active, the system shall apply cache_control: ephemeral with 5-min TTL to stable prefixes.
If summarization fails, the system shall not silently drop data; it shall keep raw messages and alert the operator.

When the user opens the dashboard, the system shall load aggregated stats within 800ms (P95).
While sessions are running, the system shall show pulse-dot animation on the running count.
Where the user has access to multiple workspaces, the system shall provide quick switch via the sidebar.
If the user tries to access a workspace without permission, the system shall return 403 and shall not render the dashboard.

tickets.title: テンプレート構造を確定 (templates/project-bootstrap/)
tickets.label: REUSE

acceptance_criteria.text: The repository shall contain templates/project-bootstrap/ with all required skeleton files (CLAUDE.md.j2, HANDOVER.md.j2, IMPLEMENTATION_PROTOCOL.md, lint-mock.sh, validate-tickets.py, settings.json).
The templates/CHANGELOG.md shall record every change to the template with version bump.
When the template structure is updated, the system shall update templates/CHANGELOG.md before merging.
If a required skeleton file is missing from templates/project-bootstrap/, the lint script shall fail.

tickets.title: WorkspaceService.bootstrap() — 新案件作成時にテンプレを GitHub repo に展開
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: When a new workspace is created via POST /api/workspaces, the system shall automatically create a GitHub repo (engine-base/proj-{slug}) and populate it with templates/project-bootstrap/ files.
When the bootstrap completes, the system shall make an initial commit with message 'chore: bootstrap project from template v{X}' on the main branch.
While the bootstrap is in progress, the system shall set workspace.status='bootstrapping' and refuse other operations until status becomes 'ready'.
Where the user provided a custom GitHub org, the system shall create the repo under that org instead of the default.
If the GitHub API call fails, the system shall mark workspace.status='bootstrap_failed', preserve the workspace row for retry, and shall not leave a partial repo on GitHub.

tickets.title: Jinja2 プレースホルダ置換エンジン
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall render every .j2 file in templates/project-bootstrap/ using Jinja2 with the workspace metadata as context.
The system shall support 10 placeholders: project_name, project_slug, client_name, deadline, phase, owner_email, tech_stack, ai_employees, template_version, generated_at.
When a required placeholder is missing from the workspace metadata, the system shall raise BootstrapError before writing any file (atomic).
While rendering, the system shall apply autoescape=False (Markdown / HTML output preserved).
If a rendered file still contains {{ }} (unrendered placeholder), the system shall fail validation and shall not commit.

tickets.title: 既存案件への遡及適用 (build-factory project migrate)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The CLI command `build-factory project migrate --workspace={id}` shall fetch the workspace's repo, diff with the latest template, and add only missing files.
When an existing file would be overwritten, the system shall skip it and report a manual-merge needed.
While dry-run mode is on (--dry-run), the system shall print the diff but shall not commit or push.
Where --all is specified, the system shall iterate through every workspace and migrate sequentially.
If the workspace repo is dirty (uncommitted changes), the system shall abort the migration and shall not force any change.

tickets.title: テンプレ更新時に全案件へ PR 自動作成 (CI 統合)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: When templates/CHANGELOG.md is updated on main, the GitHub Action shall trigger a dry-run migrate against every active workspace.
The system shall report the total number of files that would change per workspace, awaiting masato approval before proceeding.
When masato approves via the workflow_dispatch input, the system shall create a PR titled 'chore: migrate to template v{X}' on each workspace repo.
While a workspace's last migration is pending review, the system shall not create a duplicate PR for the same template version.
If any workspace migration fails (auth / network), the system shall continue with the others and report failures in the summary, not abort the whole job.

tickets.title: e2e テスト = workspace 作成 → 強制レイヤー検証
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: When the e2e test creates a new workspace, the resulting GitHub repo shall contain CLAUDE.md, docs/HANDOVER.md, scripts/lint-mock.sh, scripts/validate-tickets.py, and .claude/settings.json.
The test shall verify that grep -c 'Build-Factory' CLAUDE.md returns >= 1 in the bootstrapped repo.
When the test runs `bash scripts/lint-mock.sh` on the bootstrapped repo, it shall pass (no emojis, no AGPL, no missing files).
While running CI, this test shall execute under 60 seconds.
If any required file is missing from the bootstrapped repo, the test shall fail with a clear error indicating the missing path.
